name: IBM MQ Messaging API
what_it_does: Enterprise message queuing middleware with REST API and MQI interface — enabling organizations to reliably transfer messages between applications across platforms (mainframe, cloud, on-premises) with guaranteed delivery, ACID transaction support, IBM Cloud managed offering, and 40+ years of enterprise proven reliability.
use_cases: Integrating enterprise applications via IBM MQ REST API for reliable message exchange
Implementing transactional messaging via IBM MQ for financial transaction processing
Bridging mainframe and cloud applications via IBM MQ for hybrid integration
Enabling microservices communication via IBM MQ for guaranteed message delivery
Managing message queues via IBM MQ Admin REST API for queue operations automation
not_for: High-throughput event streaming (use Kafka for log-based streaming at scale)
Real-time pub/sub at internet scale (use Solace or AWS SNS for pub/sub)
Simple web applications needing basic queuing (use AWS SQS or RabbitMQ)
best_when: A large enterprise needs mission-critical message queuing with guaranteed delivery, mainframe integration, and decades of proven reliability — especially in banking, insurance, and healthcare.
avoid_when: You need modern event streaming, open-source without IBM licensing, or cloud-native architectures without legacy integration requirements.

auth.notes: REST API authentication: HTTP Basic auth (username:password) or API token (IBM Cloud). IBM MQ Console: browser-based management + REST API. Messaging REST API: ibm-mq-rest/v1/ endpoint for PUT/GET/DELETE messages. MQ Native MQI: channel authentication via TLS client certificates, channel access records (CHLAUTH). IBM Cloud (MQ on Cloud): IAM API key. Self-hosted: queue manager authentication (local OS users or LDAP). Admin REST API: requires Administrator authority. JWT authentication available in MQ 9.3+.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer edition: free for non-production use
pricing.paid_tiers: IBM MQ Advanced: custom enterprise; IBM Cloud Lite: free 50MB/month
pricing.notes: IBM Corporation (Armonk, NY). IBM MQ was formerly known as WebSphere MQ (renamed 2014). Original name: MQSeries (1993). Part of IBM Integration portfolio. IBM Cloud: MQ as a Service. Self-hosted: Linux, Windows, IBM i, z/OS (mainframe). z/OS: mainframe-native MQ — mission-critical financial industry workhorse. IBM MQ Advanced: includes Advanced Message Security, MQ Appliance. Licensing: per-CPU, per-core, VPCs. IBM MQ Operator: Kubernetes deployment via Kubernetes Operator. JMS support: native Jakarta Messaging (formerly JMS) support. AMQP 1.0: supported in IBM MQ. Point-to-point queuing + publish/subscribe topics. Clustering: full cluster HA and DR.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on queue manager configuration; enterprise hardware: millions msgs/sec

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for messaging; MQ native API (MQI) for full features. Basic auth or IBM IAM. Developer edition free. z/OS mainframe support. JWT in MQ 9.3+. IBM Cloud + self-hosted. CCDT client channel definition tables for connection config.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: MQ provides exactly-once delivery with transactions. REST API: message PUT/GET idempotent per correlation ID. Queue browse vs destructive GET — design for idempotency. ACID transactions: coordinated transactions with XA support.
known_agent_gotchas: Two interfaces: REST API (simple HTTP messaging) vs MQ native MQI (full feature set via SDK)
REST API requires MQ Console to be configured and running on queue manager
CCDT (Client Channel Definition Table): required for external client connections — complex configuration
Channel Authentication (CHLAUTH): whitelist-based connection control — must be configured for client access
IBM licensing: per-CPU/core/VPC — costly at scale; negotiate enterprise licensing carefully
Self-hosted vs IBM Cloud: significantly different operational models; REST API endpoints differ

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. mTLS client certificates for MQ native connections. Advanced Message Security (AMS): end-to-end message encryption/signing. FedRAMP authorized. PCI-DSS certified. z/OS: highest security platform (mainframe). IBM IAM for cloud edition. CHLAUTH: channel-level access control. 40+ years of enterprise security hardening. IBM security research and CVE response process. Self-hosted: operator responsible for security configuration. LDAP integration for enterprise identity management.
